Title: Characterization of a 4′-O-rhamnosyltransferase and de novo biosynthesis of bioactive steroidal triglycosides from Paris polyphylla

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive steroidal saponin with notable pharmacological activity, including antitumor activity as discussed for Paridis saponins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the reconstructed glycosylation module for de novo dioscin production in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in leaves for pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered N. benthamiana leaves; naturally Paridis saponins accumulate mainly in rhizome/root-associated tissues of P. polyphylla
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpUGT80A41 was included in all successful heterologous dioscin biosynthetic gene sets. 2. UGT80A41-YFP localized predominantly to both nucleus and endoplasmic reticulum. 3. The gene functions as a core non-TF, non-pathway-classified glycosylation component in the reconstructed pathway, although its precise catalytic step was not newly characterized in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a necessary module for synthetic biology reconstruction of steroidal saponin glycosylation in heterologous hosts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper did not newly biochemically characterize UGT80A41; it was used as a known pathway component during reconstitution.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization constructs; pEAQ-HT transient expression system for biosynthesis in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization in N. benthamiana; transient pathway co-expression with metabolite profiling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive steroidal saponin with pharmacological activity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineering HMGR subcellular localization to the endoplasmic reticulum increased dioscin production in the heterologous pathway
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in leaves for pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native PpHMGR was not ER-localized, unlike most other cholesterol/diosgenin pathway enzymes. 2. N-terminal truncation of 100 amino acids redirected PpHMGR to the endoplasmic reticulum. 3. Inclusion of ER-localized tPpHMGR in the pathway significantly increased dioscin production.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Subcellular retargeting of precursor-supply enzymes is an effective metabolic-engineering strategy to boost steroidal saponin yield
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes localization optimization rather than direct metabolite-specific enzymology for HMGR.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization constructs; pEAQ-HT transient expression system for biosynthesis in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization imaging; transient co-expression and metabolite quantification

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Paris polyphylla var. yunnanensis; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Paris polyphylla var. yunnanensis; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 family, PSPG motif-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sugar-chain elongation of steroidal 3-O-rhamnosyl-(1→2)-glucosides to triglycosides
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream glycosylation elongation step; branch-specific late step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to the 4′-OH of the glucose moiety in steroidal diglycosides polyphyllin V and polyphyllin VI, forming triglycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal sugar-chain elongation determinant for dioscin and related triglycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolites; Paridis saponins have notable pharmacological and antitumor activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes 4′-O-rhamnosylation of polyphyllins V and VI to produce dioscin and pennogenin 3-O-β-chacotrioside; engineered higher-activity variants improved heterologous dioscin yield
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. polyphylla roots and buds show high pathway-gene expression; dioscin was produced in engineered N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in roots and buds and in 1- to 3-year-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves for localization and pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays; transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for YFP localization construct; pEAQ-HT transient expression vector for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Illumina RNA-seq SRA: PRJNA1145501; PacBio full-length transcriptome SRA: PRJNA1145321
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome-guided candidate selection; recombinant protein enzyme assays; HPLC-DAD and UPLC-MS/MS product detection; NMR structural elucidation; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; subcellular localization; transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73DY2 was identified as the first characterized steroidal saponin 4′-O-rhamnosyltransferase from P. polyphylla var. yunnanensis. 2. It converts polyphyllin V to dioscin and polyphyllin VI to pennogenin 3-O-β-chacotrioside using UDP-rhamnose. 3. It shows strict specificity for steroidal diglycosides and UDP-rhamnose and does not glycosylate the monoglycoside trillin. 4. Its activity supports the pathway order in which 2′-O-rhamnosylation precedes 4′-O-rhamnosylation. 5. Mutants A132T, A342V, and I358T showed about two-fold improved activity, and I358T increased dioscin production in N. benthamiana to 3.12 ± 0.11 μg/g DW.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT73DY2 also accepted one non-Paris steroidal diglycoside from Ophiopogon japonicus to form a new triglycoside. H20 and D126 are implicated in catalysis; F101D abolished activity. Apparent KM values were 45.27 μM for polyphyllin V and 80.04 μM for polyphyllin VI.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear plant growth tradeoff reported; transient-expression quantification may affect reliability of absolute yields
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for synthetic biology production of dioscin and related triglycosides; activity-enhanced alleles such as I358T are useful for pathway optimization and gene stacking in heterologous hosts

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early sugar-chain elongation to generate steroidal diglycosides
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream of UGT73DY2 in sugar-chain elongation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Steroidal diglycosides such as polyphyllin V/VI-type intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2′-O-rhamnosylation of steroidal monoglycosides during Paridis saponin sugar-chain elongation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream glycosylation step enabling subsequent 4′-O-rhamnosylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolite with pharmacological value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the prerequisite diglycoside substrates required for UGT73DY2-dependent dioscin formation and contributed to de novo dioscin production in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Engineered N. benthamiana leaves; naturally P. polyphylla roots/buds for pathway expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Discussed as highly expressed in 1-year-old plants and leaves in previous work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for YFP localization construct; pEAQ-HT transient expression vector for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Used in co-expression pathway reconstruction; localization imaging; biochemical role referenced from prior study and inferred here from pathway order
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CE1 is the previously identified 2′-O-rhamnosyltransferase closely related to UGT73DY2. 2. It acts upstream by producing steroidal diglycosides that are required substrates for UGT73DY2. 3. Co-expression of UGT73CE1 was necessary in successful dioscin reconstruction in N. benthamiana. 4. Its localization overlaps with UGT73DY2 in nucleus and endoplasmic reticulum.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper did not newly re-characterize its enzymology in vitro, but it treated UGT73CE1 as a known essential pathway enzyme and compared it mechanistically with UGT73DY2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking partner with UGT73DY2 in pathway engineering for steroidal saponin production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-22 hydroxylation in the diosgenin biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key oxidative module component, not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential P450 in heterologous gene sets enabling diosgenin and dioscin production; specific combinations with DzCYP90G6 and DzCYP94N8/D109 improved yield
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization; pEAQ-HT for biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; combinatorial transient pathway reconstruction with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP90B27 localized mainly to the endoplasmic reticulum. 2. It was included in productive diosgenin/dioscin biosynthetic gene sets in N. benthamiana. 3. The paper assigns it the C-22 hydroxylation step and highlights it as part of the best-performing P450 combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study suggests endogenous N. benthamiana genes or PpCYP90B27 may contribute to C-22 oxidation contextually.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful P450 module for engineering steroidal sapogenin and saponin production in plant chassis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-16 oxidation in the diosgenin biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control component in optimal heterologous P450 combinations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Replacing PpCYP90G4 with DzCYP90G6 significantly increased diosgenin and dioscin yields in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization; pEAQ-HT for biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; combinatorial pathway co-expression and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DzCYP90G6 localized mainly to the endoplasmic reticulum. 2. It catalyzes C-16 oxidation. 3. Gene combinations containing DzCYP90G6 produced substantially higher dioscin and diosgenin than combinations containing PpCYP90G4. 4. The highest dioscin yield was obtained in combinations including DzCYP90G6.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers that using DzCYP90G6 separates C-16 oxidation from C-22 oxidation and better fits the N. benthamiana chassis context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority P450 for synthetic biology optimization of diosgenin/dioscin production

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to downstream oxidative tailoring step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-26 hydroxylation in the diosgenin biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key member of the highest-yielding P450 combination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In combination with PpCYP90B27 and DzCYP90G6, gave the highest dioscin and diosgenin production in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for localization; pEAQ-HT for biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; combinatorial transient pathway reconstruction with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DzCYP94N8 localized mainly to the endoplasmic reticulum. 2. The paper assigns it the C-26 hydroxylation step. 3. Together with PpCYP90B27 and DzCYP90G6, it produced the maximum dioscin yield before UGT73DY2 mutant optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This D. zingiberensis P450 outperformed some P. polyphylla-only combinations in the N. benthamiana chassis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for heterologous pathway optimization and interspecies enzyme stacking
